seo.title: Cabinets Plus Spokane | Complete Kitchen & Bath Renovations
seo.description: Professional design, expert installation, and guaranteed results in Spokane. From semi-custom cabinetry to stone countertops, we handle everything.

blocks.heading: Complete Kitchen & Bath Renovations in Spokane
blocks.subtext: Professional design, expert installation, and guaranteed results. From semi-custom cabinetry to stone countertops — we handle everything.

services.title: Bathroom remodel
services.description: Cabinets Plus is a trusted bathroom remodeling company in Spokane, WA, specializing in transforming bathrooms into stylish, functional spaces. From complete renovations to simple updates, our experienced team delivers thoughtful design, quality craftsmanship, and attention to every detail.

services.title: Kitchen remodel
services.description: Cabinets Plus provides professional bathroom remodeling services in Spokane, WA. We design and renovate bathrooms with a focus on functionality, clean design, and quality craftsmanship — from full remodels to targeted upgrades.

blocks.title: The difference is real:
blocks.text1: When you walk into a big-box store, you're navigating aisles of stock options with limited help.
blocks.text2:
You're buying from people **you can call directly** if anything needs attention,
not a 1-800 number three states away.

features.title: More Design Freedom, Better Value
features.description: Big Box Stores limit you to pre-made stock cabinets with minimal customization. Our semi-custom cabinets give you design flexibility without custom pricing—choose your dimensions, door styles, finishes, and hardware to fit your exact space and vision.

features.title: Expert Design, Personalized for Your Home
features.description: Expert design included. Big box stores hand you a catalog. We start with a free 3D design consultation in our Spokane showroom, where you can touch materials, see real cabinet samples, and work one-on-one with experienced designers who understand local building codes and Spokane home styles.

features.title: One Trusted Team, From Start to Finish
features.description: Big box stores can leave you managing multiple contractors and delayed timelines. At Cabinets Plus, we handle everything under one roof—from design and material selection to in-house countertop fabrication and professional installation.

blocks.trustStripContent:
You're buying from people **you can call directly** if anything needs attention,

not a 1-800 number three states away.

blocks.heading: Spokane's Premier Cabinet & Stone Showroom
blocks.subtext: Factory-direct semi-custom cabinets, granite & quartz countertops, and flooring. Experience quality before you buy.

steps.title: Explore Your Vision
steps.description: Planning a kitchen or bathroom remodel? Start by browsing cabinet styles, countertop materials, and flooring options in our Spokane showroom. Our experts help you match colors, finishes, and designs that fit both your aesthetic vision and real-world budget. Bring photos, measurements, or just ideas—we'll guide the rest.

steps.title: Free Design Consultation & Precise Measurements
steps.description: Meet with our design team to select the perfect semi-custom cabinets, granite or quartz countertops, and flooring that works for your space. We take precise measurements, discuss installation timelines, answer questions about materials, and create a detailed project plan. You'll leave with 3D renderings and a clear quote.

steps.title: Professional Installation
steps.description: Our experienced team handles full cabinet and countertop installation from start to finish. We fabricate and cut stone in-house for perfect fitment. Prefer to DIY? We provide guidance and support. *(Note: we currently specialize in cabinet and countertop installation—flooring is available for pickup or delivery.)*

steps.title: Enjoy Your Transformed Space
steps.description: Installation complete! Now enjoy your beautifully remodeled kitchen or bathroom featuring quality semi-custom cabinetry, stunning stone countertops, and premium flooring that elevate your home's style and value for years to come.

faqs.question: How much does a kitchen or bathroom remodel cost?
faqs.answer: Every remodel is different, so pricing depends on the size of the space, the materials you choose, and the scope of work. As a rough Spokane-area starting point, a typical kitchen remodel with semi-custom cabinets, stone countertops, and professional installation generally starts around $15,000–$25,000 and scales up from there based on size and finishes. Bathroom remodels typically start lower, around $8,000–$15,000. The best way to get a real number is our free design consultation — we'll take measurements, walk through your goals, and send you a detailed quote with no pressure to commit.

faqs.question: How long does a typical remodel take, from consultation to completion?
faqs.answer: Most projects fall into three phases. Design usually takes 1–2 weeks — that covers your free consultation, 3D renderings, material selection, and finalizing the quote. Material lead time runs 2–6 weeks depending on your cabinet choices and stone availability; we fabricate countertops in-house, which keeps that part fast. Installation is typically 1–2 weeks for a kitchen and 3–7 days for a bathroom. Start to finish, plan on roughly 6–10 weeks for a kitchen and 4–6 weeks for a bath. We'll give you a clear timeline at the consultation so there are no surprises.

faqs.question: Do I need to have a design ready, or do you help with that?
faqs.answer: Not at all — most of our clients walk in with nothing more than a Pinterest board, a few measurements, or just a vague idea of what they want. That's exactly what our free 3D design consultation is for. You'll sit down with our designers in the Spokane showroom, where you can touch real cabinet samples, compare countertop slabs, and see your space rendered in 3D before you commit to anything. If you do have inspiration photos or rough plans, bring them — we'll build from there. Either way, you leave with a clear design and a detailed quote.

faqs.question: How much do you charge for measurements?
faqs.answer: Measurements are included as part of our design consultation process.

faqs.question: Do you offer a warranty on your products and installation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Product warranties vary by manufacturer, and installation is backed by our workmanship standards.

faqs.question: Do you match prices?
faqs.answer: Bring us a comparable quote and we will review it with you to provide the best possible value.

faqs.question: What is your return policy?
faqs.answer: Return eligibility depends on product type and condition. Custom orders may be non-returnable.

faqs.question: How much do new kitchen cabinets cost in Spokane?
faqs.answer: For an average-size kitchen, expect $5,000–$15,000 for entry-level semi-custom cabinets and $15,000–$30,000 for higher-end semi-custom lines with premium finishes, soft-close hardware, and storage upgrades. Pricing depends on material, finish, hardware, and how much storage you build in. We give detailed itemized quotes so you know exactly what you're paying for.

faqs.question: What does "semi-custom" actually mean?
faqs.answer: Semi-custom cabinets are pre-engineered cabinet lines that let you adjust sizes, door styles, finishes, and storage upgrades within a manufacturer's options. You get far more flexibility than stock cabinets, but with shorter lead times and lower cost than fully custom builds — which makes them the best fit for most Spokane homes.

faqs.question: How long does it take to get new cabinets installed?
faqs.answer: Typical lead times run 4–8 weeks from order to delivery, plus 2–5 days for installation depending on kitchen size. We'll confirm timing before you commit so you can plan around it.

faqs.question: Can I just replace cabinet doors instead of full cabinets (refacing)?
faqs.answer: In some cases, yes. If your existing cabinet boxes are solid plywood or quality construction, refacing with new doors and drawer fronts can save significant money. If the boxes are damaged or poorly built, full replacement is usually the better long-term value. We'll evaluate during your consultation.

faqs.question: What cabinet styles are most popular in Spokane right now?
faqs.answer: Shaker remains the most popular by a wide margin — it pairs with modern, traditional, and farmhouse looks equally well. White and off-white finishes still lead, but navy, sage, charcoal, and natural wood tones are gaining ground fast, especially on islands and bathroom vanities.

faqs.question: Do you offer cabinet installation, or just sales?
faqs.answer: Both. We sell cabinets to homeowners, contractors, and DIYers, and we offer professional installation as part of our remodeling service. If you're handy and want to install them yourself, we provide guidance and how-to resources to help.

faqs.question: Are your cabinets made of real wood or particle board?
faqs.answer: We carry a range of construction qualities. Higher-end lines feature solid plywood boxes, solid wood doors, and dovetail drawer construction. Budget-friendly lines use engineered wood with veneers or thermofoil. We'll walk you through exactly what each option is built from so you know what you're buying.

faqs.question: Do you serve contractors and house flippers in the Spokane area?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. A big part of our business is supplying contractors, real estate investors, and property managers throughout Spokane and North Idaho with reliable cabinets at competitive bulk pricing. Ask about our contractor program.

faqs.question: How much do countertops cost in Spokane?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on material, square footage, and edge details. In Spokane, quartz typically runs $50–$95 per square foot installed, granite $45–$90, and quartzite or premium marble $80–$150+. We provide free, accurate quotes based on a real measurement of your space — no rough phone estimates.

faqs.question: Do you fabricate countertops in-house, or do you outsource?
faqs.answer: Everything is fabricated at our Spokane facility. Cutting, polishing, edge work, sink cutouts — it all happens here. That's why we can hit tight timelines and catch issues before they ever leave the shop.

faqs.question: What's the difference between quartz and quartzite?
faqs.answer: Quartz is engineered stone (about 90% natural quartz crushed and bound with resin), while quartzite is 100% natural stone formed from sandstone under heat and pressure. Quartz is non-porous and never needs sealing; quartzite is harder than granite but does require periodic sealing.

faqs.question: How long does countertop installation take?
faqs.answer: After you choose your slab, the typical turnaround is 1–2 weeks from measure to install. Installation day itself usually takes 4–6 hours depending on kitchen size. We work around your schedule whenever possible.

faqs.question: Do I need to seal my granite or marble countertops?
faqs.answer: Yes — natural stone (granite, marble, quartzite) needs sealing about once a year to prevent staining. We seal everything before install and walk you through how to test and reapply sealer down the road. Quartz never needs sealing.

faqs.question: Can I bring in my own slab or one from another supplier?
faqs.answer: We focus on stones we source from our trusted suppliers and our own manufactured quartz line, because we can stand behind the quality and grain. If you've found something specific elsewhere, talk to us — we may already carry it or have access to the same supplier.

faqs.question: Do you serve areas outside of Spokane?
faqs.answer: Yes. We regularly install countertops throughout Spokane County and into North Idaho — including Spokane Valley, Liberty Lake, Cheney, Deer Park, Coeur d'Alene, Post Falls, and Hayden.

faqs.question: What's the best flooring for kitchens and bathrooms in Spokane?
faqs.answer: For wet areas, we typically recommend luxury vinyl plank (LVP), SPC, or tile. They're fully waterproof, easy to clean, and stand up to the daily abuse kitchens and bathrooms see. Hardwood and laminate aren't ideal where standing water is a risk.

faqs.question: How much does flooring cost in Spokane?
faqs.answer: For materials only, expect roughly $2–$5 per square foot for laminate and basic LVP, $4–$8 for premium LVP and SPC, $6–$12 for hardwood, and $3–$10 for tile depending on size and pattern. Carpet typically runs $2–$5 per square foot. Installation is separate and varies by installer.

faqs.question: What's the difference between LVP and SPC flooring?
faqs.answer: Both are vinyl plank products. LVP (luxury vinyl plank) has a flexible PVC core, while SPC (stone plastic composite) has a rigid mineral-based core that's harder, more dent-resistant, and better for uneven subfloors. SPC tends to be the more durable choice for heavy traffic.

faqs.question: Do you install flooring?
faqs.answer: We don't currently offer flooring installation — we focus on cabinet and countertop installation. But we can point you to trusted local installers we've worked with for years, or help you find DIY-friendly products if you want to handle it yourself.

faqs.question: Can you recommend a flooring installer?
faqs.answer: Yes. We've built relationships with reliable installers across Spokane County and North Idaho who've handled jobs for our customers for years. Ask us when you visit the showroom and we'll connect you.

faqs.question: Do I need to acclimate flooring before installation?
faqs.answer: Yes — hardwood, laminate, and even some LVP products need to sit in the home for 24–72 hours before install so they adjust to indoor humidity. Skipping this step is one of the top causes of gaps, buckling, and warranty claims. Any reputable installer will plan for this.

faqs.question: Can I install flooring over my existing floor?
faqs.answer: Sometimes. LVP, SPC, and laminate can often be floated over existing tile or vinyl if the surface is flat and stable. Hardwood and tile usually require the old floor to come out. Your installer will evaluate the subfloor before starting.

faqs.question: Do you sell flooring outside of Spokane?
faqs.answer: Yes. We supply flooring to homeowners, contractors, and house flippers throughout Spokane County and North Idaho — Spokane Valley, Liberty Lake, Cheney, Airway Heights, Medical Lake, Deer Park, Coeur d'Alene, Post Falls, and Hayden.
